title: Human-input bridge: persist-as-you-type to local files, plus a Collect & Copy block any assistant can consume
context: There is no hosted app and no live agent integration: the Work HQ and the AI chat are two windows on the same workspace. The bridge between them is the load-bearing UX decision. The claude-agents cockpit went through this evolution already: it started with a big collated copy-paste block (its ADR-002 era) and refined it to server-persisted input files plus a one-line trigger phrase (its ADR-008), because the app runs a Node server with filesystem access anyway. The Personal OS Work HQ has the same server, but a different audience (non-technical, multi-assistant per G3) and an explicit user requirement for the collate-and-copy pattern on ritual screens.

options.label: A
options.summary: Pure collate-and-copy: inputs live in localStorage; a button collates every field on the screen into one formatted block to paste into chat
pros: Works with any assistant, even one that cannot read workspace files — the paste carries the full payload
Fully transparent: the user sees and can edit exactly what the AI will receive before sending
No dependency on the server being alive at consume time
cons: localStorage is not durable or inspectable — a cleared browser profile silently loses drafts
Large screens produce large pastes; chat input boxes handle them badly
Two sources of truth (browser storage vs files) — the exact problem claude-agents ADR-008 was written to kill

options.label: B
options.summary: Server-write + one-line trigger only (adopt claude-agents ADR-008 unchanged): the app saves input to docs/data/local/, the copy button copies just a trigger phrase like 'process my wrap-up'
pros: No large paste; durable, inspectable input files; single source of truth
Proven in production in the claude-agents cockpit — components port directly
cons: Requires the assistant to read workspace files on hearing the trigger — true for Copilot agent mode and Claude Code, but not guaranteed across 'any VS Code AI' (G3), and untestable assistants fail opaquely
Opaque to the user: what the AI consumes is invisible at send time, which costs trust with exactly this audience
Contradicts the explicit product requirement for a collated copy-paste block

options.label: C
options.summary: Both channels, one source of truth: debounced server-write to docs/data/local/ for durability; Collect & Copy emits the full formatted block (with a machine-recognisable header) built from that same state
pros: Meets the stated requirement and stays honest with G3: the pasted block is self-contained, so even a file-blind assistant can act on it
Durability and inspectability of ADR-008: drafts survive reloads and live in files the user (and the AI) can open
Skills accept either form — the block's header line ('## Work HQ handoff · <screen> · <date>') is also the trigger, so power users can paste just that line and let the AI read the file
Single source of truth: the block is a projection of the saved file, never a parallel store (localStorage is only an offline write-through cache)
cons: Two consumption paths to document and test in skills (full block vs trigger + file read)
Slightly more UI: the copy dialog needs to show block size and both usage hints
The formatted block format becomes a versioned contract between app and skills

decision: Adopt Option C. Every ritual screen's editable fields debounce-save through a server function to gitignored docs/data/local/<screen>.json (identical mechanism to claude-agents save-human-input.ts). The primary button on each screen is Collect & Copy: it collates the screen's saved state into a formatted markdown block opening with a recognisable handoff header, e.g. '## Work HQ handoff · wrap-up · 2026-07-05', followed by the labelled responses. Skills (chief-of-staff, os-helper) are amended to recognise a handoff block pasted in full AND the bare header line as a trigger to read docs/data/local/ directly — same behaviour either way, because the file and the block carry identical content. The handoff block format is specified in docs/specs/file-contracts.md alongside the section contracts (ADR-P6-002).
consequences: Per OQ-003 (resolved): the reference consumer is GitHub Copilot in the VS Code native chat UI, reading AGENTS.md — Claude Code availability is NOT assumed. Both consumption paths (full pasted block; bare trigger + file read) are acceptance-tested against Copilot agent mode; the full-block paste doubles as the safety net for assistants whose file access is unverified. README and onboarding carry a note that the as-is configuration assumes Copilot, guiding the user's AI assistant to adapt the setup for a different chosen assistant. docs/data/local/ is gitignored from day one (raw human drafts are never committed — only what the AI formalises into OS markdown). Skills gain a short 'Work HQ handoff' section each; that is the full blast radius on the AI side. The block format is versioned in file-contracts.md, so app and skills can evolve it deliberately. If experience shows everyone uses the trigger-only path, a future ADR can retire the full-block copy without touching storage.
costCapacity: No new recurring cost. Writes are local filesystem only.
